response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack and systems software developer with a strong web, tooling, and open-source background, but the resume shows no evidence of the core requirements for this role: SoC/IP modeling, hardware performance architecture, AI accelerator or GPU workload analysis, or hardware-software co-design. The closest relevant experience is low-latency DLang services and some C/C++ IDE component work, which is far from principal-level silicon performance architecture. While total years of experience satisfy the BS + 8 years path, the domain mismatch is fundamental rather than a gap that could be closed quickly.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded contexts
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) handling millions of requests per second
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and technical community teaching
concerns: No SoC, IP, or hardware modeling experience of any kind
No deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator background (also missing SIMD/MIMD exposure)
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not performance architecture at the hardware-software boundary

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI/deep-learning or GPU workload expertise
Low-latency systems and performance-minded engineering background
